# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent missing vulnerability scan.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

# GitHub Actions Workflow

name: Deployment Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

  build-and-deploy:
    needs: build-and-test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build artifacts
        run: npm run build

      - name: Deploy to production
        run: |
          echo "Deploying to production..."
          # Add deployment commands here, e.g., using SSH or a CI/CD tool like AWS CodeDeploy
